data.title: SoFi Stock Falls as Wells Fargo Initiates Hold Rating, KBW Reaffirms Bearish Stance after Short Seller Report
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock was down more than 3% on Thursday morning after Wells Fargo initiated coverage of the fintech company with a Hold rating, citing valuation concerns. Also, on Wednesday, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods (KBW) reaffirmed a Sell rating on SOFI stock, noting concerns raised by short seller Muddy Waters. Notably, Muddy Waters called SoFi "a financial engineering treadmill, not a healthily growing origination business." 

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Additionally, the decline in SOFI stock on Thursday was due to broader market concerns about rising oil prices amid tensions in the Middle East.

Wells Fargo and KBW Analysts Weigh in on SoFi Stock

Wells Fargo analyst Donald Fandetti initiated coverage of SoFi Technologies stock with a price target of $19. The 5-star analyst believes that SOFI is a "digital leader sitting at the nexus of technology and financial services/payments convergence." He also noted the company's solid revenue and earnings per share (EPS) growth. That said, he prefers to be on the sidelines given the stock's high valuation and loan-sale risk.

Meanwhile, KBW analyst Tim Switzer reiterated a Sell rating on SOFI stock with a price target of $20. Switzer noted that Muddy Waters' short report reflects an in-depth analysis of SoFi's accounting practices, revealing some previously unknown information from Uniform Commercial Code filings and bills of sale, alongside the company's regular investor disclosures.

Switzer believes that most of Wall Street is already aware of these issues, which implies that the stock's movement going forward is likely more tied to retail investors, who are less aware of the intricacies of the company's accounting practices and business model. Switzer also thinks the short report sparked some new questions.

It is worth noting that SoFi disputed Muddy Waters' accusations, calling its report "factually inaccurate and misleading."

Is SOFI Stock a Good Buy?

Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on SoFi Technologies stock based on five Buys, eight Holds, and three Sell recommendations. The average SOFI stock price target of $25.42 indicates 53% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 3/19/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading lower on Thursday as tensions in the Middle East continue to ramp higher.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Iran has targeted several key oil infrastructure sites in the Middle East, further disrupting the flow of oil as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. The effects of higher oil and gas prices are widespread and could slow economic growth by fueling higher inflation, increasing transportation costs, and squeezing consumer spending.

New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears

Rate Cut Odds Plummet amid Intensifying Geopolitical Tensions

In the latest sign of escalation, President Trump is considering the deployment of ground troops to Iran, according to Reuters. The troops could assist with escorts through the Strait of Hormuz or participate in an operation to secure Iran's ​enriched uranium stockpile.

Elsewhere, rising inflation risks have battered rate cut odds. The Fed is now expected to maintain interest rates until year-end, with the CME FedWatch tool assigning 73.5% odds to this scenario, up from 47.1% a day ago.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Lululemon (LULU) Founder Chip Wilson Is Still Unhappy After Board Changes: 'Deficiencies Remain'
data.description:
Lululemon Athletica (LULU) founder Chip Wilson remains unhappy after the Canadian retailer announced a longtime board member won't stand for re-election, saying that "deficiencies remain" at the company.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wilson said that while he is pleased David Mussafer will step away from the Vancouver-based retailer after 14 years on the board of directors, he still feels that "glaring governance deficiencies remain." He added that the appointment of former Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) CEO Chip Bergh to Lululemon's board to replace Mussafer is "underwhelming."

Wilson, who has been agitating for change on the Lululemon's board for months, recommends the company appoint three of his nominee suggestions: On Holding AG (ANON) co-chief executive Marc Maurer, former ESPN chief marketing officer Laura Gentile, and former Activision chief executive Eric Hirshberg.

Criticisms of Lululemon

Wilson wasn't impressed by Bergh's appointment because he said the new board member led several companies that struggled for years compared to their peers.

For starters, Wilson said the board still has three directors tied to a private equity firm led by Mussafer. Wilson has said the chairman and managing partner of private equity firm Advent International Corp. "long impeded change" at Lululemon.

He said he expects Mussafer to step back from any involvement in new board appointments.

"It would be highly unusual and inappropriate for a retiring director to be guiding or actively involved in strategic matters," Wilson said in a Wednesday statement.

Wilson built Lululemon into an athleisure juggernaut and fashion status symbol for much of the 2000s, but it has struggled in recent years to keep up with trends, generate the same excitement as competitors Alo and Vuori and stop the erosion of its stock price.

Though Wilson hasn't been an executive or board member for years and is estranged from the retailer, he still holds some of its stock.

Wilson began pushing for an overhaul at the company around the same time that former Lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald announced that he would step down at the end of January.

However, Lululemon, which has yet to comment on Wilson's latest criticisms, has accused him of preventing it from interviewing his suggested board members by demanding the company sign an agreement before it proceeds. It has not outlined what terms it alleges Wilson is requesting.

Prior to joining Levi Strauss & Co., Lululemon said Bergh spent 28 years at Procter & Gamble and has been part of HP Inc., e.l.f. Beauty Inc. and Pinterest Inc.'s boards.

Wilson felt Bergh's appointment was also underwhelming because the Lululemon board had indicated highly qualified directors declined to stand for nomination until the company's ongoing proxy battle is over.

As Wilson's attacks on Lululemon continue, the company is still in search of a replacement for McDonald, who is set to take the helm of beauty conglomerate Wella Co. in April.

Lululemon chief financial officer Meghan Frank and chief commercial officer André Maestrini are splitting the CEO role on an interim basis.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Tesla Stock (TSLA) Is Down Today and Why This Analyst Calls It a 'Big Deal'
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares slipped about 3% on Thursday as investors reacted to a regulatory escalation that could carry far-reaching implications for the company's self-driving ambitions.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the day, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration upgraded its probe into Tesla's Full Self-Driving system from a Preliminary Evaluation to a more advanced Engineering Analysis, now covering more than 2.4 million vehicles.

That shift is critical because it marks a transition from early-stage information gathering into a formal process where regulators actively test vehicles, analyze crash data in depth, and scrutinize engineering decisions behind the system. The probe is focused on whether Tesla's camera-based approach can properly detect and respond to reduced visibility conditions, such as fog, glare, or dust, where perception becomes more difficult. Rather than a narrow software issue, the question now centers on whether the underlying design can reliably recognize when it should not be operating at full capability.

GLJ Research analyst Gordon Johnson argues that this escalation carries far more weight than many investors may assume, describing it as a "Big Deal." The analyst explains that while a Preliminary Evaluation is essentially regulators "sniffing around," an Engineering Analysis represents confirmation that a pattern exists and triggers the agency's full investigative toolkit, including vehicle testing and deeper engineering interrogation. According to Johnson, this stage sits just one step before a mandatory recall, making it a pivotal moment rather than a procedural formality.

New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears

The analyst also stresses that the issue at hand is not easily resolved, noting that the investigation targets a potential limitation of Tesla's vision-only system in degraded visibility environments. In Johnson's view, that raises a systems-level concern rather than a simple bug that could be addressed through an over-the-air update, which has been Tesla's typical approach in past recalls. If regulators conclude that the architecture itself is insufficient, the implications could extend beyond a quick fix and require more fundamental changes.

More broadly, Johnson ties the development directly to Tesla's valuation, arguing that a significant portion of the company's worth is linked to expectations around a global robotaxi network powered by its self-driving software. The analyst contends that the Engineering Analysis challenges that assumption at its core, stating that scaling such a network becomes difficult if the software faces the risk of being restricted or recalled.

Johnson is a well-known TSLA bear, but what does the rest of the Street think of the stock? TSLA carries a Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating based on 31 analyst opinions, including 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and 7 Sells. At the same time, the average 12-month price target of $399.25 implies ~5% upside from current levels. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: ZipRecruiter Stock (ZIP) Sinks despite Embracing OpenAI's ChatGPT — What's Happening?
data.description:
Shares in ZipRecruiter (ZIP), the operator of a marketplace that connects job seekers and employers, plunged about 5% early Thursday. This comes despite the launch of the ZipRecruiter app for ChatGPT, which adds its job search service directly to the app store of OpenAI's popular AI assistant.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Bringing ZipRecruiter into generative AI tools opens a novel way to drive conversations between job seekers and employers," noted Ian Siegel, ZipRecruiter's co-founder and CEO. With this move, users can leverage ChatGPT to find job opportunities available on ZipRecruiter's marketplace, the company stated.

However, the plunge in ZIP shares on Thursday likely indicates that jitters about the potential of AI to disrupt several industries persist. It also comes as analysts remain largely bearish on ZipRecruiter's stock, recently lowering their price targets.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Micron (MU) Stock Plunges after Q2 Earnings — But Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Micron (MU) shares trended lower in early trading hours on Thursday despite reporting strong fiscal Q2 results, as investors focused on the company's higher capital spending plans. Despite the pullback, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom tool suggests retail investors remain confident in the stock, continuing to add Micron to their portfolios.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Market Reacts to Micron's Earnings

Micron's results were strong, with earnings and revenue beating expectations, driven by solid AI demand and tight supply in memory markets.  The company posted earnings of $12.20 per share on revenue of $23.9 billion, both well ahead of expectations.

However, the stock reaction reflected concerns about rising investment levels. The company raised its capital expenditure outlook to exceed $25 billion in 2026 and signaled an even larger step-up in 2027, as it builds capacity to meet AI demand. This weighed on sentiment, even as the broader outlook for memory remains strong.

The reaction also pulled down other semiconductor stocks, including SanDisk (SNDK) and Western Digital (WDC), as investors reassessed valuations after Micron's update.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

Micron remains widely held among retail investors. According to TipRanks data, about 4.9% of portfolios hold MU, with an average allocation of 7.65%.

More importantly, activity trends remain positive:

  • Portfolios holding MU increased 0.6% over the last seven days
  • Holdings rose 5.2% over the last 30 days

Investor sentiment also remains "Very Positive," indicating continued confidence despite the recent pullback.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Gold Down 5% Today, 3/19/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) has failed to behave like a safe haven amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and is down by over 5% on Thursday. In addition, the precious metal usually benefits from expectations of higher inflation. Higher gas and oil prices from the U.S.-Iran war and disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz have raised the risk of inflation. However, gold has fallen 8% since the start of the conflict on February 28.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A major factor sending gold lower is falling rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit from lower rates, as the opportunity cost of holding it relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, goes down.

Trade QQQ with leverage

Gold Faces Headwinds from Profit-Taking and Strong U.S. Dollar

When the war began, the most favored outcome at 33.7% was for the Fed to cut rates twice this year, according to the CME FedWatch tool. Now, the most likely outcome is for zero rate cuts by year-end with 72.8% odds.

Furthermore, investors may be taking profits following a historic run in the precious metal. With the S&P 500 (SPX) down by 4% over the past month, capital could be rotating into the index and out of gold. Meanwhile, the U.S. Dollar Index has risen 1.75% during the past month, putting additional pressure on gold as a stronger dollar makes the metal more expensive for overseas buyers.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: SpaceX Doubles Down on Nvidia (NVDA) Chips Amid Musk's AI Expansion Plans
data.description:
SpaceX, the private space exploration company led by Elon Musk, plans to continue large-scale orders of Nvidia (NVDA) AI chips. This commitment supports the company's expanding AI initiatives following its acquisition of xAI. The announcement, made by Musk on March 18, 2026, highlights ongoing reliance on Nvidia hardware for compute-intensive tasks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla (TSLA), also under Musk's leadership, will maintain similar large-scale Nvidia chip purchases despite advancing its own in-house AI processors.

SpaceX Commits to Large-Scale Nvidia Chip Purchases

Elon Musk confirmed on March 18, 2026, that SpaceX AI, the entity resulting from SpaceX's February 2026 acquisition of xAI and Tesla, expects to keep ordering Nvidia AI chips at scale. This commitment comes even as Tesla develops its next-generation AI5 chip, optimized for edge computing in applications like autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots. While the announcement signals ongoing reliance on Nvidia's hardware, it did not include details on specific order volumes or total spending.

Trade NVDA with leverage

Notably, Musk described Nvidia as a company he greatly admires, praising its rapidly growing valuation. Nvidia GPUs, including H100 and Blackwell series, have already powered xAI's Grok model training and continue to support SpaceX's AI workloads in space technology and data processing.

Musk Balances Nvidia Reliance with In-House AI Development

SpaceX's AI push intensified after acquiring xAI in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $1.25 trillion in combined equity, integrating AI models with satellite infrastructure such as Starlink for potential orbital data centers. Musk has emphasized space-based AI as a future low-cost compute solution amid terrestrial power constraints.

This recent commitment to Nvidia sustains demand for the chipmaker's products, boosting investor confidence in NVDA amid broader AI growth. For the private company SpaceX, it ensures continued progress in AI-driven innovations without immediate disruption from in-house alternatives.

Is NVDA a Strong Buy?

Although some investors are still hesitant to buy NVDA stock, it likely isn't due to the company's recent performance or sales. Nvidia remains the top global AI chip maker, holding a dominant market share of 70% to 95%. Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks have given the stock a "Strong Buy" consensus rating, with an average price target of $274, implying roughly 54% upside.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Shares in Kraft Heinz (KHC) Rise on Hopes of Shareholders Re-Heating Unilever Food Merger
data.description:
Shares in consumer goods giant Kraft Heinz (KHC) edged higher today on reports that shareholders might not be ready to give up on a megamerger of its food brands with Unilever (UL) after initial discussions led to no agreement.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ketchup and Mayo

It was reported by the Financial Times that the two firms had discussed a potential combination of Unilever's food brands, such as Marmite and Hellmann's mayonnaise, with Kraft Heinz's condiments business such as Heinz Ketchup in recent months. However, those talks have since ended.

If successful, it would have seen the brands come under the same ownership, creating a new entity worth tens of billions of dollars.

Both companies have been busy looking at the structure of their business in recent months. Kraft Heinz had been planning to split its company into separate businesses, with one global entity focusing on sauces, spreads, and seasonings, while the other would sell grocery staples in North America. Through this move, KHC aimed to create businesses that are more focused and less complex.

However, last month chief executive Steve Cahillane rowed back on the decision alongside a commitment to spend $600 million across marketing, sales and R&D.

New Twist to the Tale

Earlier this week, Unilever was reported to be in the early stages of considering a separation of its food assets and is speaking with advisers as it studies future options. That would have come in the wake of the demerger of the Magnum Ice Cream Company MICC -0.85% ▼ from Unilever back in December.

"Hot on the heels of demerger talk for Unilever's food assets, there's a new twist to the story. Reports suggests Unilever has held talks with Kraft Heinz to merge their food assets into a combined entity, creating a powerhouse for ketchup and mayonnaise," said Dan Coatsworth, head of markets at AJ Bell. "Essentials in homes around the world, there is plenty of competition for these food items, yet Unilever and Kraft own the big brands. That makes the prospect of a merger more interesting."

He said the brands stand to prosper in stronger economic conditions and could achieve economies of scale for purchasing raw materials and operational savings.

 "Reports suggest talks are no longer live, but the idea of a combination has now been planted and that might prompt shareholders on both sides to push for more information on how a merger could work, and if it's worth pursuing," Coatsworth said.

Is KHC a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, KHC has a Moderate Sell consensus based on 9 Hold and 4 Sell ratings. Its highest price target is $26. KHC stock's consensus price target is $23.38, implying a 5.36% upside.
data.author: David Craik
